And this is something I'm continuing to work on and work on for myself as well, but I wanted to share it with you because doing it from day one is way better than trying to play catch up. Right? One thing that I really want you all to include in your brand positioning is that you're going to be selling stuff to your audience. Right? What do you stand for? I stand for selling stuff because I'm running a business. I don't want you building these huge audiences of freebie seekers. I want you making it clear early on that you're going to be making offers to people that are going to help them. If people don't like that, they can leave. One of the core pillars of your brand's positioning needs to be that you're going to sell people stuff that will help them. You need your audience to become trained and accustomed to the fact that you are going to sell to them. Otherwise, if you spend six months and never try and sell to them, you're going to build up this audience of people. And then when you try to sell, they're going to freak out. They're going to go, oh, this is weird. I'm not used to. You know, I had that, I experienced that a little bit. I spent a long time just delivering value and trying to build up. And then you start selling things. And it's a big shift. This has been a learning curve for me as well. But if you're starting from scratch, build into your brand positioning, I'm going to make you offers. I'm going to sell you stuff. You don't have to buy them. It'll be stuff that'll help you. It's in your best interest to do it. It's going to put you on the path of least resistance. It's going to get you closer to your results. But I am going to sell you stuff because I'm running a business. Right? I'm not just putting out content here delivering

## [12:11 - 13:16]

value because I enjoy doing it or it's good for my health. I'm doing it because I'm running a business and I'm showing you what I know what I'm doing. So that is one mandatory component of your brand positioning is that you tell people that you are going to sell them stuff. Right? You don't have to flat out be like, look guys, welcome my group. I'm going to sell you stuff. But just start making them offers. Have offers sitting in your group. This was a big learning curve for me. If you'd asked me this a couple of years ago, I would have said the flip side. I would have said not deliver value. But the more and more I dive into this, you've got to be forward with people that you're going to be selling. People are going to spend money either way. It's just a matter of if they spend it with you or they spend it with someone else. And if your content is good and it's helpful, people are still going to hang around even if the offers are there. So don't stress about that. Hopefully you found that helpful. That's kind of a 101 introduction to the concept of brand positioning. Start looking into these questions.
